behaviors.description: Plugin with missing required manifest field is rejected before code execution
behaviors.justification: Manifest validation is pure, deterministic logic on JSON/object input. Mocking the plugin code executor reliably prevents execution of rejected plugins. No I/O, filesystem, or external dependencies required to catch this failure.
behaviors.test_description: A plugin whose manifest.json lacks a required field (e.g., name, version, or dependencies) causes the loader to reject it with a validation error, and the loader never executes any code from that plugin—verified by spy/mock assertions that the plugin code path is not invoked

behaviors.description: Plugin dependency order is respected: dependent plugin loads after its dependencies
behaviors.justification: Dependency resolution and hook invocation order is deterministic orchestration logic. Mock plugins with onLoad spies reliably capture call order without touching the filesystem. The failure (wrong order) is pure logic, not I/O related.
behaviors.test_description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B in its manifest, the loader invokes plugin B's onLoad hook before plugin A's onLoad hook—verified by asserting the order of spy/mock calls on each plugin's onLoad function

behaviors.description: Circular dependency is detected and both plugins in the cycle are not loaded
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection is a graph algorithm on manifest dependency metadata. No execution or I/O is needed—pass mock/stub manifests to the validation function and verify it returns a cycle error. Confirms that no plugins in the cycle enter the load phase.
behaviors.test_description: When two plugins declare a circular dependency on each other, the loader detects the cycle, returns an error identifying it, and loads neither plugin—verified by asserting that the loader rejects the configuration and neither plugin's onLoad was called

behaviors.description: Plugin onLoad hook is only called after entry module has been imported and executed
behaviors.justification: Verifying 'and executed' requires observing actual module runtime effects (side effects, exports, state initialization). Mocking the import hides real failures: module syntax errors, missing dependencies, or failed initialization are not caught by Unit-level mocks. Real require/import from disk confirms the module actually executes before onLoad is invoked.
behaviors.test_description: A plugin's onLoad hook is not invoked until the entry module has been successfully required and executed, verified by checking that module-level side effects (global state, exports) exist before onLoad is called and by using a real test module on disk

behaviors.description: Plugin error during onLoad does not block initialization of unrelated plugins
behaviors.justification: Error handling and continuation logic is deterministic orchestration code. Mock plugins with onLoad functions that throw exceptions reliably simulate failure modes. Verify remaining independent plugins load despite the error. No I/O or real execution needed to test error path logic.
behaviors.test_description: When plugin A's onLoad hook throws an error, plugin B (which does not depend on A) completes its onLoad hook successfully, and the loader reports the error from A without terminating—verified by spy/mock assertions that B's onLoad was called and the error was logged
